claim: The zodiac and astronomical sections (entropy 3.7149 and 3.7471 bits respectively, lowest of all sections) encode a label-and-numeral system where recurring short words function as positional labels (month names, star names, degree markers) rather than running prose, and these labels follow a fixed template grammar with â‰¤3 syntactic slots, explaining low entropy through high repetition of a small closed vocabulary.
evidence_for: Zodiac section has lowest entropy (3.7149 bits) and smallest word count per folio (~108 words/folio vs. 84 for herbal)
Astronomical section also has low entropy (3.7471) and very small total corpus (872 words, 8 folios)
Low entropy in label-heavy sections is consistent with a closed vocabulary of repeated positional labels
Current beliefs at 0.645 and 0.616 support homophonic/label-oriented encoding in zodiac/astronomical sections
Zodiac manuscripts of the period commonly used repetitive label structures around circular diagrams
evidence_against: If labels follow fixed templates, type-token ratio should be very low in zodiac â€” but overall hapax ratio remains high corpus-wide
The astronomical section's 872 words across 8 folios may simply reflect sparse text rather than a structurally distinct encoding
test: Extract all words from zodiac and astronomical sections. Compute the type-token ratio (TTR) and compare to herbal and recipes sections. Identify all words appearing â‰¥3 times within zodiac/astronomical only. Fit a positional template model: for each word position within a 'label unit' (defined by folio layout), compute conditional entropy H(word | position). Compare H(word | position) in zodiac vs. herbal sections.
test_metric: Type-token ratio for zodiac/astronomical vs. herbal/recipes sections; conditional entropy H(word|position) within label units in zodiac
test_threshold: Confirm if zodiac TTR <0.45 (vs. expected >0.60 for prose) AND H(word|position) in zodiac is at least 0.3 bits lower than in herbal; deny if zodiac TTR â‰¥0.55 or positional entropy shows no reduction

reasoning: If the zodiac/astronomical sections can be shown to encode a closed label vocabulary with template grammar, they become the highest-priority decipherment target â€” a small closed vocabulary with known referents (months, stars, degrees) is far more tractable than open prose.
